title: Performance Metrics and Monitoring Quiz
description: Test your knowledge about the critical role of performance metrics and monitoring in modern web applications.

questions.question: What is 'Latency' in the context of web performance?
options: The number of bytes in a request.
How long it takes for a single request to be fulfilled by the server.
A type of database indexing.
A way to automatically compress files.

questions.question: Why are 'Percentiles' (P95, P99) generally better than 'Averages' for measuring performance?
options: Because they are easier to calculate.
Because averages can hide outliers; a small number of very slow requests can make your P99 look terrible while your average remains fine.
Because percentiles are supported by all browsers.
None of the above.

questions.question: Which of these is a popular open-source tool for collecting and storing time-series metrics?
options: Express.js
Prometheus
Mongoose
React Router

questions.question: What's the purpose of a '/health' endpoint?
options: To check if the user is feeling well.
A simple route that returns a 200 OK status if the application and its critical dependencies are functioning correctly.
To automatically generate a backup of the data.
To improve the user experience.

questions.question: What is 'Distributed Tracing' Useful for?
options: To trace the user's location.
To follow a single user request's path across multiple independent microservices to find performance bottlenecks.
A type of database indexing.
A way to automatically compress files.
